transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-06-27]
[00:00] You: How did the customer call go?
[00:15] Speaker 1: It went okay. They mentioned they might want a custom integration with Salesforce.
[00:45] You: Did they commit to anything?
[00:55] Speaker 1: Not really. They said someone on their team might reach out about pricing.
[01:20] Speaker 2: I heard from the account manager that the customer is considering upgrading their plan.
[01:50] You: Interesting. Let's keep an eye on it.
[02:00] Speaker 1: Yeah. I think we should wait to hear from them before doing anything.
[02:20] You: Agreed. I'll follow up with the account manager next week to check in.

adversarial_injections.description: Customer mentioned a possible Salesforce integration but made no commitment — it belongs to the customer, not to any meeting participant
'Someone on their team might reach out about pricing' is speculative with no owner on our side
'Customer considering upgrading' is second-hand information — no commitment from any meeting participant

action_items.description: Follow up with the account manager to check in on the customer
action_items.source_quote: I'll follow up with the account manager next week to check in.
